subparts.text: What do you mean by gradient of a scalar? CO 1
Derive relationship between E and V. CO 1
Derive Gauss’s law in differential form. CO 2
Derive the point transformation among cartesian, cylindrical and spherical coordinates variables with the help of suitable figures. CO 1

The temperature in an auditorium is given by, T = x2 + y2 – z. A mosquito at in the auditorium desires to fly in such a direction that it will get warm as soon as possible. In what direction must it fly? CO 2
Find the Laplacian of the following scalar fields i) 𝑉1 = [𝑥2 + 𝑦2 + 𝑧2 ] 3 2 ⁄ ii) 𝑉2 = 5 𝑟2𝑠𝑖𝑛 θ cos φ CO 2
Determine which of the following are genuine electric fields. i) 𝐸1 = 5 𝑥3 𝑎𝑥+ 15 𝑥2 𝑦 𝑎𝑦 ii) 𝐸2 = 5 𝑟𝑠𝑖𝑛 θ cos φ 𝑎𝑟 CO 1
Derive law of refraction of the electric field at a boundary free of charge. CO 2
Let D = 2xy 𝑎𝑥 + x² 𝑎𝑦 C/m² find The volume charge density 𝜌𝑣 The flux through the surface 0 < x < 1, 0 < y < 1, z = 1 CO 2
